description_long: This object is a pair of ornate, ceremonial swords displayed upright on decorative stands. Each sword features a broad, straight blade with a pointed tip and a central fuller, finished in a metallic silver with gold accents along the edges and at the tip. The blades have a large circular cutout near the base, adding a unique design element. The handles are short and integrated into elaborate, gold-accented stands that resemble chalices, with a flared guard and a rounded base. The stands are tiered and circular, providing stability and a sense of grandeur. The overall appearance suggests these swords are intended for display or ritual use rather than practical combat.
description: A pair of ornate, gold-accented ceremonial swords with broad blades and decorative stands, featuring circular cutouts near the base of each blade.
description_view_0: Two swords are shown from the front, highlighting the broad blades, gold detailing, and circular cutouts near the base, as well as the elaborate stands.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the thin profile of the blades and the layered, rounded bases of the stands.
description_view_2: Another front view, similar to view 0, showing the symmetrical design and decorative features of both swords and stands.
description_view_3: A side view from the opposite direction, again emphasizing the slimness of the blades and the ornate, tiered bases.
attribute_blade_type: straight, double-edged, broad blade with central fuller
attribute_handle_design: short, integrated with ornate gold-accented stand, flared guard, rounded base
attribute_ornamentation: gold accents, decorative patterns, circular cutout in blade
